{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_288939f56ca344e38a191c53","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tDOM verification for canvas topic cards (bead adc-1rdt).\n3\t\n4\tCompanion to ``tests/test_canvas_render.py`` (the render-contract test). It drives\n26\tthe REAL production canvas module (``src/canvas/canvas.js``) through the Node DOM\n27\trunner (``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js``) that adc-1l8w introduced, then queries\n28\tthe emitted ``outerHTML`` with a tiny stdlib-only DOM parser. how Playwright locators query the DOM: find elements\n52\t# by tag + class + attribute, then read their text.\n53\t\n54\t\n55\tclass _Node:\n56\t    \"\"\"One element node in the parsed tree.\"\"\"\n57\t\n58\t    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n59\t\n60\t    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n61\t        self.tag = tag\n62\t        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n63\t        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n64\t        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n65\t        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n66\t        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n67\t\n68\t    def _walk(self):\n69\t        for child in self.children:\n70\t            yield child\n71\t            yield from child._walk()\n72\t\n73\t    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None,\n74\t                 attrs: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n75\t        \"\"\"All descendants matching tag / class / exact attribute values.\"\"\"\n76\t        out: list[_Node] = []\n77\t        for node in self._walk():\n78\t            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n79\t                continue\n80\t            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n81\t                continue\n82\t            if attrs and any(node.attrs.get(k) != v for k, v in attrs.items()):\n83\t                continue\n84\t            out.append(node)\n85\t        return out\n86\t\n87\t    def find(self, **kw) -> \"_Node | None\":\n88\t        matches = self.find_all(**kw)\n89\t        return matches[0] if matches else None\n90\t\n91\t    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n92\t        return class_ in        for node in self._walk():\n99\t            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n100\t        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n101\t\n102\t\n103\t# Void elements never push onto the \"wbr\"}\n106\t\n107\t\n108\tclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n109\t    def __init__(self) -> None:\n110\t        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n111\t        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n112\t        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n113\t\n114\t    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n115\t        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n116\t        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)\n117\t        if tag not in _VOID:\n118\t            self._stack.append(node)\n119\t\n120\t    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n121\t        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n122\t\n123\t    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n124\t        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n125\t            if self._stack[i].tag == verification for canvas topic cards (bead adc-1rdt).\n\nCompanion to ``tests/test_canvas_render.py`` (the render-contract test). Where\nthat file asserts the rendered *HTML string*, this file queries the rendered DOM\nthe way a browser test would — by selector — and verifies the things a canvas\nconsumer relies on to locate and read a card:\n\n- **data attributes**: every card exposes ``data-topic-id`` AND\n  ``data-topic-type`` on its root (added in src/canvas/canvas.js for this bead),\n  so cards are locatable by id and by type with a robust, stable selector.\n- **layout classes**: the card skeleton is present — ``topic-card`` (root),\n  ``topic-header`` (the card header), ``topic-label``, ``topic-type``, and the\n  result body (``result-content`` / ``result-summary``) when there is a result.\n  (The bead's AC names these as card / card-header / card-body; in this canvas\n  they are topic-card / topic-header / result-content — the idiom the existing\n  markup already uses. Both names map to the same elements below.)\n- **text content**: the label, the result summary, the type-badge text and the\n  urgency-badge text all match the data handed to ``createTopicCard()``.\n- **every topic type**: project, research, personal, exception, compound each\n  render with the right ``data-topic-type`` and type-badge.\n- **negative cases**: an empty card list renders nothing; a card with no result\n  renders no result body; one card's topic id never bleeds into another's.\n\nThis runs **headlessly and hermetically** — no browser, no live server. It drives\nthe REAL production canvas module (``src/canvas/canvas.js``) through the Node DOM\nrunner (``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js``) that adc-1l8w introduced, then queries\nthe emitted ``outerHTML`` with a tiny stdlib-only DOM parser. Playwright's own\n``expect()``/locator API can't launch a browser on this NixOS host (its bundled\nchromium is missing ~26 FHS libraries), so this is the suite that stays green\nhere; the Playwright counterpart lives in\n``tests/e2e/test_canvas_dom_verification.py`` and runs wherever a browser can.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom html.parser import HTMLParser\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import node_available, render_card, render_cards\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n)\n\n\n# --- minimal selector-style DOM query over stdlib html.parser ------------------\n#\n# BeautifulSoup/lxml aren't (and shouldn't need to be) a dependency, so we parse\n# the well-formed, simple HTML createTopicCard() emits with the standard library.\n# This deliberately mirrors how Playwright locators query the DOM: find elements\n# by tag + class + attribute, then read their text.\n\n\nclass _Node:\n    \"\"\"One element node in the parsed tree.\"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n\n    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n        self.tag = tag\n        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n\n    def _walk(self):\n        for child in self.children:\n            yield child\n            yield from child._walk()\n\n    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None,\n                 attrs: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n        \"\"\"All descendants matching tag / class / exact attribute values.\"\"\"\n        out: list[_Node] = []\n        for node in self._walk():\n            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n                continue\n            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n                continue\n            if attrs and any(node.attrs.get(k) != v for k, v in attrs.items()):\n                continue\n            out.append(node)\n        return out\n\n    def find(self, **kw) -> \"_Node | None\":\n        matches = self.find_all(**kw)\n        return matches[0] if matches else None\n\n    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n        return class_ in self.classes\n\n    @property\n    def text(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Own text plus all descendant text, whitespace-collapsed.\"\"\"\n        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n        for node in self._walk():\n            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n\n\n# Void elements never push onto the open-element stack.\n_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n\n\nclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n\n    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)\n        if tag not in _VOID:\n            self._stack.append(node)\n\n    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n\n    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n            if self._stack[i].tag == tag:\n                del self._stack[i:]\n                break\n\n    def handle_data(self, data):\n        s = data.strip()\n        if s:\n            self._stack[-1].text_parts.append(s)\n\n\ndef parse_card(card_out: dict) -> _Node:\n    \"\"\"Parse one ``render_card``/``render_cards`` output dict into a query tree.\n\n    The node returned is the card root (``<div class=\"topic-card ...\">``), so\n    callers can ``.find(class_=\"topic-label\")`` directly.\n    \"\"\"\n    root = _TreeBuilder()\n    root.feed(card_out[\"outerHTML\"])\n    root.close()\n    # The card root is the first real element under the synthetic root.\n    assert root.root.children, \"rendered card had no root element\"\n    return root.root.children[0]\n\n\ndef render(card: dict) -> _Node:\n    \"\"\"Render a card dict through the real canvas.js and parse it for querying.\"\"\"\n    return parse_card(render_card(card))\n\n\n# --- card-shape factory (mirrors TopicCard.to_dict()) -------------------------\n\n\ndef _level(seconds: int) -> str:\n    if seconds < 600:\n        return \"fresh\"\n    if seconds < 3600:\n        return \"stale\"\n    return \"very-stale\"\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str | None = \"3 pods running\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    data: dict | None = None,\n    seconds: int = 5,\n    latest_result: dict | None | object = ...,  # sentinel\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build a card dict in the exact shape GET /topics returns.\"\"\"\n    if latest_result is ... and summary is not None:\n        latest_result = {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency, \"data\": data}\n    elif latest_result is ...:\n        latest_result = None\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\n            \"id\": topic_id,\n            \"label\": label,\n            \"type\": topic_type,\n            \"project_slugs\": [],\n            \"scope\": \"session\",\n            \"session_id\": \"s-1\",\n            \"created_at\": 0,\n            \"last_active\": 0,\n            \"archived_at\": None,\n            \"result_count\": 1,\n        },\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": _level(seconds)},\n        \"latest_result\": latest_result,\n    }\n\n\n# === data attributes ==========================================================\n\n\nclass TestDataAttributes:\n    def {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c58662b32bd34c5a80e9842e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tTest data injection client for canvas testing.\n3\t\n4\t``TestDataInjector`` is an async httpx client that injects test sessions and\n5\ttopics into a running ADC server (or an in-process FastAPI app) and cleans them\n6\tup afterwards. It is transport-agnostic:\n7\t\n8\t- **Live server** (used by Playwright/browser e2e suites against :8000):\n9\t    ``TestDataInjector(base_url=\"http://localhost:8000\")``\n10\t- **In-process** (no network server, isolated temp DB — used by unit tests):\n11\t    ``TestDataInjector(app=src.main.app)``\n12\t\n13\tEndpoints exercised:\n14\t\n15\t- ``POST   /api/v1/sessions``                 — create a session w/ predictable ID\n16\t- ``DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}``     — cascade-clean a session (teardown)\n17\t- ``GET    /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics`` — read back injected topic cards\n18\t- ``POST   /api/v1/test/create-topic``         — inject a topic + result directly (no LLM)\n19\t- ``POST   /api/v1/test/dispatch``             — inject via the real dispatch pipeline (LLM)\n20\t\n21\tThe direct ``create-topic`` path is deterministic and network-free; ``dispatch``\n22\texercises the full router → fetch+synthesize pipeline but depends on the ZAI LLM\n23\tproxy. Default injection uses ``direct`` so tests stay fast and hermetic.\n24\t\"\"\"\n25\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n26\t\n27\timport uuid\n28\tfrom logging import getLogger\n29\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n30\t\n31\timport httpx\n32\t\n33\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n34\t\n35\tDEFAULT_BASE_URL = \"http://localhost:8000\"\n36\t# All auto-generated session IDs share this prefix so they are easy to spot and\n37\t# bulk-clean, and never collide with real production session IDs.\n38\tTEST_SESSION_PREFIX = \"test-inject-\"\n39\t_TESTSERVER = \"http://testserver\"  # synthetic host used with the ASGI transport\n40\t\n41\t\n42\tclass InjectionError(RuntimeError):\n43\t    \"\"\"Raised when an injection/cleanup HTTP call fails or returns an unexpected shape.\"\"\"\n44\t\n45\t\n46\tclass TestDataInjector:\n47\t    # pytest sees any ``Test*`` class as a test class; this is a helper, not a\n48\t    # test. Suppress collection so the suite stays warning-free.\n49\t    __test__ = False\n50\t\n51\t    \"\"\"Async httpx client for injecting and tearing down canvas test data.\n52\t\n53\t    Tracks every session it creates and deletes them all on ``cleanup()`` /\n54\t    ``aclose()``, so tests can use it as a context manager for guaranteed\n55\t    teardown:\n56\t\n57\t        async with TestDataInjector(app=app) as inj:\n58\t            await inj.create_session(\"test-inject-foo\")\n59\t            await inj.inject_topic(\"test-inject-foo\", label=\"Pods\")\n60\t            ...  # cleanup runs on exit, even if the body raises\n61\t    \"\"\"\n62\t\n63\t    def __init__(\n64\t        self,\n65\t        base_url: str = DEFAULT_BASE_URL,\n66\t        *,\n67\t        app: Any = None,\n68\t        client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None,\n69\t        timeout: float = 30.0,\n70\t    ) -> None:\n71\t        if app is not None and base_url != DEFAULT_BASE_URL:\n72\t            # Avoid ambiguous combos — pick one transport explicitly.\n73\t            raise ValueError(\"Pass either app= (in-process) or base_url= (live), not both\")\n74\t\n75\t        self._owns_client = client is None\n76\t        self._created_session_ids: list[str] = []\n77\t\n78\t        if client is not None:\n79\t            self._client = client\n80\t        elif app is not None:\n81\t It is transport-agnostic:\n\n- **Live server** (used by Playwright/browser e2e suites against :8000):\n    ``TestDataInjector(base_url=\"http://localhost:8000\")``\n- **In-process** (no network server, isolated temp DB — used by unit tests):\n    ``TestDataInjector(app=src.main.app)``\n\nEndpoints exercised:\n\n- ``POST   /api/v1/sessions``                 — create a session w/ predictable ID\n- ``DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}``     — cascade-clean a session (teardown)\n- ``GET    /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics`` — read back injected topic cards\n- ``POST   /api/v1/test/create-topic``         — inject a topic + result directly (no LLM)\n- ``POST   /api/v1/test/dispatch``             — inject via the real dispatch pipeline (LLM)\n\nThe direct ``create-topic`` path is deterministic and network-free; ``dispatch``\nexercises the full router → fetch+synthesize pipeline but depends on the ZAI LLM\nproxy. Default injection uses ``direct`` so tests stay fast and hermetic.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\nDEFAULT_BASE_URL = \"http://localhost:8000\"\n# All auto-generated session IDs share this prefix so they are easy to spot and\n# bulk-clean, and never collide with real production session IDs.\nTEST_SESSION_PREFIX = \"test-inject-\"\n_TESTSERVER = \"http://testserver\"  # synthetic host used with the ASGI transport\n\n\nclass InjectionError(RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when an injection/cleanup HTTP call fails or returns an unexpected shape.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass TestDataInjector:\n    # pytest sees any ``Test*`` class as a test class; this is a helper, not a\n    # test. ) -> None:\n        if app is not None and base_url != DEFAULT_BASE_URL:\n            # Avoid ambiguous combos — pick one transport explicitly.\n            raise ValueError(\"Pass either app=    def _resolve_session_id(cls, session_id: Optional[str]) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Normalize any session-id input into a prefixed, trackable test ID.\n\n        Guarantees every session this injector -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def create_session(self, session_id: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Create a test session. Auto-generates a prefixed ID if none given.\n\n        Explicit labels are namespaced under the test prefix (see\n        :meth:`_resolve_session_id`) so they stay easy to spot and bulk-clean.\n        Idempotent: calling twice with the same input returns ``created: false``\n        the second time. Each spec is a kwarg dict for inject_topic.\"\"\"\n        results = []\n        for spec in specs:\n            results.append(await self.inject_topic(session_id, **spec))\n        return results\n\n    async def inject_session_with_topics(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_specs: list[dict],\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convenience: create a session, then inject N topics into it.\"\"\"\n        sid = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        await self.create_session(sid)\n        topics = await self.inject_topics(sid, topic_specs)\n        return {\"session_id\": sid, \"topic_count\": len(topics), \"topics\": topics}\n\n    async def get_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Return the canvas topic cards for a session (GET .../topics).\"\"\"\n        session_id = self._resolve_session_id(session_id)\n        resp = await self._client.get(f\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics\")\n        self._check(resp)\n        data = resp.json()\n        return data.get(\"cards\", [])\n\n    # -- teardown ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    async def cleanup(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Delete every tracked session. Best-effort: never raises.\n\n        Returns the list of session IDs actually removed.\n        \"\"\"\n        removed: list[str] = []\n        for sid in list(self._created_session_ids):\n            try:\n                await self.delete_session(sid)\n                removed.append(sid)\n            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — teardown must be best-effort\n                logger.warning(f\"cleanup: failed to delete session {sid}: {exc}\")\n                self._untrack(sid)\n        return removed\n","numLines":294,"startLine":1,"totalLines":294}}}

-------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\n\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\nclass ShimNode {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n    }\n\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n    }\n\n    get className() {\n        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n    }\n\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) {\n                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n            },\n            contains(c) {\n                return self._classes.includes(c);\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    get dataset() {\n        return this._dataset;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._textContent;\n    }\n\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v data- prefix).\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        return at call time.\nglobal.document = {\n    createElement(tag) {\n        return new ShimNode(tag);\n    },\n};\n\n// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel (process.argv[2] !== undefined && process.argv[2] !== \"\") {\n        return JSON.parse(process.argv[2]);\n    }\n    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n    return In Node, expose them for the headless DOM test.\n98\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n99\t    module.exports = {\n100\t        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n101\t        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n102\t        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n103\t        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n104\t    };\n105\t}\n106\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ad630bd8-6bca-40eb-862c-94aec196a2e7","uuid":"e06202fb-dee2-4722-9217-42b861e602ab","timestamp":"2026-07-21T06:04:12.208Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/canvas/canvas.js","content":"/*\n * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element. They are kept in this separate, loadable module (rather\n * than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be verified headlessly\n * — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js loads this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and asserts the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM test.\n */\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${topic.type || 'adhoc'}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text;\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM test.\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n    module.exports = {\n        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n    };\n}\n","numLines":106,"startLine":1,"totalLines":106}}}

It imports Playwright\\n   lazily and skips cleanly when there is no server or no launchable browser, so\\n   it collects here and runs for real wherever a browser exists.\\n\\n## Production change\\n\\n`src/canvas/canvas.js` `createTopicCard()` now sets `card.dataset.topicType =\\ntype || 'adhoc'`, exposing `data-topic-type` alongside the existing\\n`data-topic-id`. Both suites assert the attribute is present, on the root, and reflects\\nthe type (defaulting to `adhoc` when the topic has no type).\\n\\n## AC → suite mapping\\n\\n| Acceptance criterion | Where covered |\\n|---|---|\\n| Query cards with Playwright locators | `tests/e2e/test_canvas_dom_verification.py` (`page.locator('[data-topic-id=...]')`) |\\n| Data attributes exist (`data-topic-id`, `data-topic-type`) | `TestDataAttributes` (both suites) |\\n| Text content matches injected data | `TestTextContent` (label, summary, type/urgency badge) |\\n| Layout classes present (card / card-header / card-body) | `TestLayoutClasses` — maps to `topic-card` / `topic-header` / `result-content`, the existing canvas idiom |\\n| Pass when correct, fail when missing/malformed | `TestNegative` + mutation check (below) |\\n| All topic types (project, research, personal, exception, compound) | `TestAllTopicTypes` (parametrized over all 5) |\\n| Negative tests (missing topics not rendered) | `TestNegative` (empty list → 0 cards; no card-body without a result; no unrelated type; id non-leak; malformed label) |\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py` → **26 passed**.\\n- `tests/e2e/test_canvas_dom_verification.py` → **14 collected, 14 skipped**\\n  (no server/browser on this host; skips, never errors at import — unlike the\\n  older top-level-`import playwright` navigation test).\\n- No regression in suites sharing `canvas.js`/inject infra:\\n  `test_canvas_render.py`, `test_inject.py`, `test_canvas_sse_render.py` →\\n  **32 passed**.\\n- **Mutation proof of \\\"fail when malformed\\\"**: removing the new\\n  `card.dataset.topicType = typeClass` line fails 7 tests\\n  (`KeyError: 'data-topic-type'`); restoring returns to 26 green.
